text:
Here is the passage:

Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. Think of it like cleaning and reorganising a messy room; everything is still in the house, but it is much easier to find things. The main goal is to enhance readability and maintainability. When code is cluttered or confusing, it becomes difficult for developers to understand and modify it. This problem is often called technical debt. If you ignore technical debt, it grows over time, making future changes slow and risky. By regularly refactoring, you pay down this debt, keeping the code clean and easy to work with. Ultimately, refactoring creates a healthier, more efficient codebase.
